uiIssues.description: Image 5 (src: "https://promos.makemytrip.com/notification/xhdpi/Roadblock-386x540-LongWeekend-A") has no alt attribute or an empty one.
uiIssues.impact: Screen readers announce this image as "image" with no context, making it meaningless to visually-impaired users. Search engines also use alt text to index images, so missing alt text reduces SEO value.
uiIssues.recommendation: Add a descriptive alt attribute that conveys the purpose or content of the image. For purely decorative images use alt="" (empty string) to tell screen readers to skip it.
uiIssues.selector: div#header-container > div:nth-of-type(2) > div:nth-of-type(2) > div > div > div > div > img

uiIssues.description: Link 1 (href: "none") has no visible text and no aria-label.
uiIssues.impact: Screen readers read this link as "link" with no destination hint. Search engines treat anchor text as a relevance signal — an empty link passes no keyword value.
uiIssues.recommendation: Provide descriptive anchor text or an aria-label that describes the destination. Avoid generic text like "click here".

uiIssues.description: Input field 1 (type="text") has no associated <label>, aria-label, or aria-labelledby attribute.
uiIssues.impact: Screen readers cannot tell users what to type into this field. This is a WCAG 2.1 Level A violation (Success Criterion 1.3.1 and 3.3.2).
uiIssues.recommendation: Add a <label for="inputId"> element whose for attribute matches the input's id. If a visible label is not desired, use aria-label on the input itself.
uiIssues.selector: div#header-container > div:nth-of-type(2) > div:nth-of-type(2) > div > section > form > input:nth-of-type(1)

uiIssues.description: Two interactive elements overlap: input and input at 1280×800.
uiIssues.impact: When clickable elements overlap, users may accidentally trigger the wrong action. On touchscreens the problem is amplified.
uiIssues.recommendation: Use browser DevTools to identify the overlapping elements and fix their positioning (check z-index, absolute/fixed positioning, or negative margins).
